data.title: Huawei Receives Frost & Sullivan's 2025 Global Technology Innovation Leadership Recognition in Telecom DC Power
data.description: Huawei has been acknowledged for revolutionizing the telecom energy infrastructure by enhancing the functionality through cloud-based intelligent battery control. This innovation has propelled the company to the forefront of the distributed energy systems market, solidifying its position as a market leader. The recognition was made public in San Antonio on March 30, 2026, according to a recent announcement from Frost & Sullivan.

data.title: Arbitrum's price near $0.09 may recover to $0.12 with RSI at 40.71. Bulls eye a break above $0.10 amid downside risks to $0.08.
data.description: The trading price of ARB is hovering around $0.09 with a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 40.71. Analysis indicates a possible upward movement towards the $0.12 resistance level if buyers manage to surpass the $0.10 mark. However, there are risks of a decline towards the $0.08 support level.

data.title: Aptos (APT) expected to reach $1.50 by mid-2026, currently trades at $0.94 with RSI at 45.22. Resistance at $0.99.
data.description: The stock of APT is currently trading at $0.94, with a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 45.22. According to technical analysis, there is an immediate resistance expected at $0.99. Long-term forecasts indicate a target price of $1.50 by the mid-2026 recovery period.

data.title: Tryg - Transactions in connection with share buyback programme
data.description: Tryg A/S initiated a share buyback programme on January 22, 2026, where the Board of Directors approved purchasing shares worth up to DKK 1.0 billion. This programme follows guidelines set by the EU Market Abuse Regulation, EU Regulation no. 596/2014, and Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 (the “Safe Harbour Regulation”). The share buyback programme is scheduled to conclude by May 13, 2026.

data.title: Stock Market Today: Small Caps, Techs Lead Gains; This Nuclear Play Breaks Out But Micron Sinks (Live Coverage)
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index showed an increase as President Donald Trump proposed a peace plan to Iran. Following this news, oil prices experienced a significant drop. During the day, small-cap and tech stocks led the gains in the market. Additionally, there was a breakout observed in the nuclear sector, while Micron faced a decline. The market activity was covered live by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Draganfly Stock Sheds 21% Post-Earnings — Here's Why It's Still A Buy
data.description: Shares of the drone technology company Draganfly Inc. (NASDAQ: DPRO) experienced a sharp decline on Wednesday following disappointing fourth-quarter earnings results. The stock plummeted over 21% following the report, which revealed a wider loss than anticipated and a revenue miss compared to Street estimates. The company reported a quarterly loss of 24 cents, a significant deviation from the expected loss of 13 cents. Revenue also fell short by nearly 19%, leading to a shift in short-term investor sentiment. Gross margin contracted to approximately 17%, raising concerns about profitability. Despite the negative market reaction, the report and future guidance still present a speculative Buy opportunity for investors comfortable with a turbulent market. For more detailed information, please visit the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Ondas, GameStop, PDD and More Stocks With Earnings This Week
data.description: The earnings season is nearly wrapping up, with a few popular retail picks still set to report this week. ONDS stock has seen an increase post-earnings. Ondas Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: ONDS) unveiled its quarterly results with a forecast for fiscal 2026 revenue of no less than $375 million. Although the drone tech company posted a loss of 36 cents per share (higher than the estimated five cents), its revenue stood at $30.1 million, surpassing the expected $27.6 million. WeRide Inc. (NASDAQ: WRD) also disclosed its Q4 results, showing a smaller-than-predicted loss per share and revenue surpassing expectations. On Tuesday, March 24, after market close, all eyes are on GameStop Corp. (NYSE: GME) as it releases its fourth-quarter results.

data.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Up As Trump Says This; Cathie Wood Snaps Up IPO Stock Amid 84% Dive (Live Coverage)
data.description: Following President Trump's remarks on Iran, the Dow Jones experienced a boost. A pharmaceutical company debuted on the stock market with a test entry today. Noteworthy investor Cathie Wood made stock purchases. The latest report on the stock market indicates the Dow having an upward trend, with Trump's announcement being a significant factor. Additionally, Wood's activity in acquiring IPO stocks, despite an 84% drop, has drawn attention.
